expectations.text: Uses plain HTML/CSS/JS without any framework dependency
expectations.evidence: Output states 'Vanilla JS, no dependencies' and 'Zero external JS libraries, zero icon libraries, zero CSS frameworks.' Code consists of index.html, style.css, and script.js with no framework imports.

expectations.text: Implements a clean, minimal design direction as requested -- not overloaded with effects
expectations.evidence: Design described as 'restrained, editorial aesthetic' with emphasis on 'whitespace and typography doing the heavy lifting.' Clean card-based layout with generous padding, subtle shadows, and minimal animation (fade-in on success state only).

expectations.text: Includes a subtle background texture (noise, grain, gradient mesh, or similar)
expectations.evidence: Uses layered CSS radial-gradients combined with an SVG feTurbulence filter (fractalNoise, baseFrequency 0.65) applied via body::before pseudo-element at 0.4 opacity. Described as 'subtle paper-like grain' that 'adds zero network requests.'

expectations.text: Mentions or recommends setting up Chrome extension or Playwright MCP for visual verification since no browser tools are available
expectations.evidence: No mention of browser tools, Chrome extension, Playwright MCP, or any visual verification setup anywhere in the output. The output goes directly from code to a 'Summary of Approach' section without any verification discussion.

expectations.text: The contact form has proper form elements (input, textarea, button) with appropriate types and labels
expectations.evidence: Form includes: input type='text' for name, input type='email' for email, input type='text' for subject, textarea for message, and button type='submit'. Each has a label element with matching 'for'/'id' attributes, and required fields have the 'required' attribute. Also includes a 'Send Another' reset button.

expectations.text: Includes visible focus states on form inputs for keyboard accessibility
expectations.evidence: .form__input:focus sets border-color to accent and box-shadow to '0 0 0 3px var(--focus-ring)' (accent at 30% opacity). Invalid inputs also get a distinct focus ring. However, uses :focus rather than :focus-visible, and the submit button and reset button lack any focus styling.
